meta.title: ClearK12 Sales Hub
meta.description: Sales enablement content for ClearK12: Crystal Instruction and Crystal Writing (LoneStar CR in Texas; Crystal Writing in Ohio & Georgia).
meta.source: Synced from ClearK12 Sales Brand & Messaging Guide + ClearK12 Sales Playbook (Word, v1.0). Stage benchmarks + nurture motion and OH/GA state editions are drafts pending sales-enablement approval. Unverifiable 10x/2-hour claims removed 2026-07-21. Crystal outreach touch kit (persona: campus) merged 2026-07-21.

company.name: ClearK12
company.positioning: ClearK12 is an educational technology company built for the realities of today's classrooms. We make the day-to-day work of teaching measurably easier — turning student data and required standards into fast, confident instructional action. Our products sit alongside the curriculum, assessments, and systems districts already own; we make those investments work harder rather than asking schools to rip and replace.
company.promise: Educational technology that helps teachers do their best work — with less friction.

brandPrinciples.title: State standard-built
brandPrinciples.body: Designed for real classrooms and accountability — not repackaged national content.

brandPrinciples.title: Teacher-first
brandPrinciples.takeaway: We remove work from teachers' plates.
brandPrinciples.body: Less prep, planning, and grading — never more.

brandPrinciples.title: Additive, not disruptive
brandPrinciples.takeaway: We work alongside what districts already own.

brandPrinciples.title: Speed to action
brandPrinciples.takeaway: We compress the time between “what happened” and “what to do next.”
brandPrinciples.body: Data is everywhere; the value is responding faster.

brandPrinciples.title: Plain-spoken
brandPrinciples.takeaway: Name the buyer's real pain in their words.
brandPrinciples.body: No edtech jargon, no buzzword soup.

company.voice: Confident, warm, and practical. We sound like an experienced educator who respects the buyer's time and knows the classroom. We are optimistic without overpromising, and specific without drowning people in features. Sentences are short. Claims are concrete.
company.goldenRule: Lead with the problem the buyer feels, not the features we ship. Features prove the story — they are never the opening line.
logoRules: Keep clear space around the logo equal to the height of the “K12” mark; never crowd it.
Place the full-color logo on white or very light backgrounds; use a reversed (white) logo on navy.
Do not stretch, recolor, add effects, or place the logo on busy photos.
company.paletteNote: Use the ClearK12 palette for corporate materials, capabilities decks that span both products, contracts, and the company signature line. Product materials lead with the product palette and carry ClearK12 as the endorsing parent (“a ClearK12 product”).
company.emailSignature:
First Name Last Name | Title
cleark12.com
P: phone number
company.introLine: Hi, I'm _______ with ClearK12. I'd like to share one of our programs with you, Crystal Instruction.
company.familyNote: The platforms are complementary, not competing. Crystal Instruction is about instructional response across Math, ELAR, and Science; Crystal Writing, including LoneStar CR, is a specialist in the single hardest-to-grade skill. Many districts are strong candidates for both.

crystalWriting.name: Crystal Writing
crystalWriting.tagline: A system for developing strong writers.
crystalWriting.blurb: The writing development hub that provides scaffolding and support to help students build the skills to become strong writers.
crystalWriting.availability: Available in Texas, Ohio, and Georgia
crystalWriting.positioning: Crystal Writing is the writing partner for classrooms. Using advanced AI, teachers create standards-aligned response tasks in seconds, students write with individualized scaffolding, and every response is scored instantly with personalized feedback — built specific to state standards and grade-level writing expectations.
crystalWriting.mission: Helping teachers grow confident writers — by making writing practice frequent, scaffolded, and instantly scored, without the grading burden.

states.name: Texas
states.statusTip: Approved source content from the Brand & Messaging Guide and Sales Playbook.
states.note: Built exclusively for TEKS and Texas writing.

states.name: Ohio
states.statusTip: Full messaging and playbook adapted from the approved Texas copy; pending sales-enablement sign-off.

states.name: Georgia
states.statusTip: Full messaging and playbook adapted from the approved Texas copy; pending sales-enablement sign-off.

crystalWriting.stateNote: The writing story is the same everywhere — the standards, test names, and product name change by state. Texas sells as LoneStar CR; every other state sells as Crystal Writing. Each state below has its own overview, messaging, and playbook.
bestFitSignals: State-test writing / constructed-response performance is a named priority.
ELA teachers overloaded with writing grading; low writing-practice volume.
Grades 3–8 and high-school English focus; interest in AI built to the state's standards.

shared.name: Crystal Writing
shared.oneLiner: The constructed-response writing partner for classrooms.
shared.marketTagline: A system for developing strong writers.
shared.mission: Helping teachers grow confident writers — by making writing practice frequent, scaffolded, and instantly scored, without the grading burden.
shared.positioning: Crystal Writing is the constructed-response writing partner for classrooms. Using advanced AI, teachers create standards-aligned constructed-response tasks in seconds, students write with individualized scaffolding, and every response is scored instantly with personalized feedback — built specifically to each state's standards and writing expectations.
shared.voice: Confident, credible, and evidence-led — Crystal Writing pairs bold, benefit-led claims ('Write smarter, learn faster') with a strong research spine, adapted to each state's standards and assessments. It speaks plainly to the grading pain teachers feel every week and to the state-test outcomes leaders own.

valuePillars.title: More writing practice
valuePillars.body: Students write far more when every response no longer waits on hand-grading.

valuePillars.title: Instant scoring & feedback
valuePillars.body: Students get a grade and personalized feedback immediately; teachers stop hand-grading.

valuePillars.title: Built for standards
valuePillars.body: Constructed response aligned to what the state actually requires.

valuePillars.title: Automated, multi-tiered scaffolding
valuePillars.body: Support auto-adjusts to each student's level.

valuePillars.title: Time back for teachers
valuePillars.body: Teachers stop hand-grading every response.

shared.problem: It is unsustainable for teachers to grade writing and provide feedback in a way that is timely and meaningful for students. Students need far more writing reps than they get, because every response a teacher assigns is another stack to grade by hand. So practice stays low, feedback comes days late, and writing scores lag. The bottleneck isn't willingness — it's grading time.
shared.problemTagline: The bottleneck to better writing isn't effort — it's grading time.

steps.name: Assign
steps.body: Teachers access unlimited standards-aligned constructed-response tasks in seconds, with strategy anchors like RACE built in.

steps.name: Write
steps.body: Students write with automated scaffolding matched to their level, plus self-paced lessons on writing fundamentals.

steps.name: Score
steps.body: AI scores every response instantly and returns personalized, actionable feedback — no manual grading.

steps.name: Revise & Grow
steps.body: Students revise across drafts (where the research shows the biggest gains) while teachers watch growth in Crystal-Clear reports.

isIsnt.is: A specialist writing-practice and instant-scoring tool that dramatically increases practice volume and returns teacher time.
isIsnt.isnt: A full ELA curriculum, a general essay checker, or a replacement for the teacher's judgment. The teacher stays in control of instruction; Crystal Writing removes the grading bottleneck.

audienceMessaging.leadMessage: Assign more writing, grade none of it — and give every student instant feedback.
More writing practice in every classroom — with visible, tracked growth.
A research-backed way to raise constructed-response scores at scale.

sayThis: Built specifically to each state's standards and writing expectations.
Assign more writing, grade none of it.
More writing practice, without more grading.
Instant, personalized feedback on every response.
Research-backed writing growth in daily routines.
notThis: Works for any state / any test
Grades like a human, so no need to review
bestFitSignals: State-test writing/constructed-response performance is a named priority (ECR).
ELA teachers overloaded with writing grading; low writing-practice volume.
Grades 3–8 and high school English focus; interest in AI that's built to the state's standards.
discoveryQuestions: How is constructed response (ECR) going on the state test — and in daily practice?
Realistically, how often do students write, and how long until they get feedback?
How much time are ELA teachers spending grading writing each week?
shared.proofIntro: Frequent writing, explicit strategies, and instant feedback across drafts map directly to the research base. Use these to substantiate claims; cite responsibly.
proofPoints: Strategy-rich writing instruction improved achievement by up to +1.14 SD (Graham & Perin, Writing Next, 2007).
Automated writing evaluation produced a +0.86 SD effect, strongest when students revised across drafts (Zhai & Ma, 2023).
Immediate automated feedback improved writing performance by +0.55 SD across grade levels (Fleckenstein et al., 2023).
Daily argument writing raised scores +0.21 on a 1-6 rubric across 47 districts (SRI, C3WP, 2017).
shared.productClaims: Instant scoring and personalized feedback on every response, built specifically to each state's standards and writing expectations, grounded in the peer-reviewed research above.
shared.hook: Assign more writing, grade none of it — and give every student instant feedback.

scripts.coldCall: Hi, this is ___ with Crystal Writing. The quick reason I'm calling is that state ELA assessments lean hard on constructed-response writing — and it's the hardest thing to practice at volume, because every response a teacher assigns is another stack to grade. Crystal Writing is built specifically to each state's standards and writing expectations. Teachers create constructed-response tasks in seconds, students write with scaffolding matched to their level, and every response is scored instantly with personalized feedback — so students write far more, and teachers get their grading time back. Would it be worth a quick 20-minute demo to see how this could work for your ELA teachers?

coldEmail.subject: More writing practice, zero grading, at [District]
coldEmail.body:
Hi [Name] — state tests lean hard on constructed response, but students can only write as much as teachers can grade. That's the bottleneck.

Crystal Writing is the constructed-response writing partner for classrooms. Built to state standards, it lets teachers generate unlimited writing tasks, gives students instant AI scoring and personalized feedback (with RACE/TAG strategy anchors and auto-scaffolding), and takes the grading stack off teachers' desks — so students can write far more, far more often.

Worth a 20-minute look for [District]? — [Rep]

pitchOutline: Problem (45s): state tests made constructed response central; practice is capped by grading time; feedback comes days late.
Who we are (45s): the constructed-response writing partner, built specifically to each state's standards and writing expectations — a specialist, not a curriculum.
Write. Score. Grow. (1 min): assign in seconds, students write with scaffolding, instant scoring + feedback, revise across drafts.
Proof (1 min): frequent writing, explicit strategies, and instant feedback are the research-backed levers (Writing Next; AWE meta-analyses).
Impact (1 min): dramatically more practice, grading time returned to teachers, tracked growth in Crystal-Clear reports.
Close (15s): more writing, none of the grading — the fastest path to better writers.

objections.objection: Can AI really grade writing accurately?
objections.response: It scores instantly and consistently against the rubric, and teachers stay in control — you can review and adjust. The win is volume and speed of feedback, where the research shows the biggest gains.

objections.objection: Is this just an essay checker like the free tools?
objections.response: No — it's built exclusively for each state's standards and constructed response, with auto-scaffolding, strategy anchors like RACE/TAG, and district analytics. Generic tools aren't aligned to what the state test requires.

audiences.label: District administrators
audiences.note: Superintendents, C&I / academics, assessment, MTSS, and technology leaders. Primary economic buyer.

audiences.label: School / campus leaders
audiences.note: Principals and assistant principals. Strong champions; own campus outcomes.

audiences.label: Teachers
audiences.note: The users and the most persuasive internal champions.

process.principle: Sell the way districts buy: validate what they own, name the pain they feel, and prove we make their people's week easier.
process.stagesIntro: Seven stages, from first touch to expansion. Don't advance a deal until it meets the exit criteria — that's how we keep the pipeline honest.
process.icp: Both products serve public school districts and campuses. Primary buyers are district administrators (curriculum/C&I, academics, assessment, MTSS, and technology leaders) and campus leaders (principals and APs). Teachers are the users and powerful internal champions.
process.fitNote: Before investing in a full demo cycle, confirm FIT. If two or more are unclear, stay in discovery.
process.discoveryIntro: Discovery is where deals are won. Ask, listen, and mirror their words back in the demo. Start shared, then go product-specific.
process.cadenceNote: Re-enter engaged-but-not-ready contacts into the long-tail nurture below and re-time around their budget/planning cycle.

nurture.intro: Most K-12 deals die on timing, not fit. After the Day-22 break-up email, engaged-but-not-ready contacts move into nurture — treat it as a real pipeline stage with its own motion, not a graveyard.
rhythm: Monthly value touch — rotate formats: a research stat, a win from a similar district, a new-feature note, or a state-assessment news item. Never 'just checking in.'
Quarterly personal touch — a short call or personalized email tied to something real: their scores, their board agenda, a leadership change.
Every touch gets logged with a next-trigger date in the CRM — nurture only works if the next action has a date.
triggers: Budget planning window (roughly Jan–Apr for the next school year) — re-open the conversation before budgets lock.
State test results release (STAAR early summer; OST and Georgia Milestones over the summer) — a writing or growth dip is a natural re-entry.
Back-to-school priority reset (Aug–Sep) — new initiatives get named; map ours to theirs.
Leadership change — a new superintendent, C&I lead, or principal resets the conversation; champions who move districts take us with them.
New money — grants, Title reallocations, or a board-approved initiative that names our pain.
nurture.rule: Engaged-but-not-ready is a stage, not a dead end. The rep who is already present when budget season opens wins the re-entry.

process.livingNote: This is a living document. Bring what's working (and the objections you can't beat) to sales enablement so we improve it together.

stages.name: Prospect
stages.goal: Build a targeted list of districts/campuses in your state that fit.
stages.exit: Named contact + a hypothesis for why this account has the pain.
stages.stall: Pipeline coverage below ~3× target → rebalance time toward prospecting.

stages.name: Connect
stages.goal: Earn a first conversation (cold call, email, referral, event).
stages.exit: A scheduled discovery call or demo.
stages.stall: Cadence exhausted with no conversation → move to nurture; don't let it sit in Connect.

stages.name: Discover
stages.goal: Understand their systems, pain, priorities, and buying process.
stages.exit: Confirmed pain, decision-makers, timeline, and rough budget path.
stages.stall: 3+ weeks without confirmed pain and decision-makers → re-run FIT or escalate to your manager.

stages.name: Demo
stages.goal: Show the specific workflow that solves their stated pain.
stages.exit: Champion confirms fit and names remaining stakeholders.
stages.stall: 2+ weeks and the demo won't schedule → re-engage the champion with two specific times and a deadline.

stages.name: Propose
stages.exit: Proposal reviewed; objections surfaced; next step to decision set.
stages.stall: 30+ days without movement → manager touch; re-anchor to their budget window.

stages.name: Close
stages.goal: Navigate approval (budget, PO, procurement) and sign.
stages.stall: 60+ days or a missed board date → reconfirm the procurement path; reset to the next board meeting.

stages.name: Onboard & Expand
stages.goal: Fast, successful rollout; then grow (more campuses, second product).
stages.exit: Live usage + a named expansion or renewal path.
stages.stall: No named expansion or renewal path by day 90 → schedule the growth conversation.

process.cycleNote: A healthy end-to-end cycle runs roughly 3–6 months. Spring deals close for a fall start; fall deals usually close in the spring budget cycle. Rule of thumb: a deal that has doubled its stage benchmark is stalled — act on it, don't wait on it.
process.timingNote: K-12 buying runs on budget cycles and procurement rules everywhere we sell. Spring (planning next year) and late summer are prime; know the district's fiscal year and its purchasing path — ESCs and purchasing co-ops in Texas, ESCs in Ohio, RESAs in Georgia — and whether a specific approval path applies.

items.title: Fit & Pain
items.body: Is there a real, named problem we solve (instructional response for Crystal; writing/grading for LoneStar CR)?

items.title: Influence
items.body: Who decides and who else must say yes (C&I, principal, tech, business office)? Do we have a champion?

items.title: Timing & Money
items.body: Is there a budget path and a decision window (fiscal year, grant, Title funds, ESSER-replacement, board timeline)?

discoveryShared: What are your top academic priorities this year, and how are you measuring progress?
What systems do teachers already use for curriculum, assessment, and data? What's working — and where does it break down?
Where does the most teacher time get lost right now?
Who's involved in a decision like this, and what does your approval process look like?

cadence.action: Cold call; if no connect, send the cold email tailored to the product/pain.
Value follow-up: one concrete outcome (time saved / faster response / more writing) + demo ask.
Second call attempt; reference the emails; offer two specific demo times.
Share the product one-pager or a short demo video; light social proof from similar Texas districts.
Third call; try early morning or after dismissal when admins are reachable.
'Am I aimed at the right person?' — ask for a referral to the correct owner.
Break-up email: close the loop respectfully, leave the door open, note the seasonal planning window.

demoTips: Open with their stated pain in their words — then show the one workflow that solves it. Don't tour every feature.
Use a scenario that matches their grade band/subject (multiplication reteach for Crystal; an ECR prompt for LoneStar CR).
Show the teacher time saved and the student experience — that's what champions repeat to leadership.
Confirm fit out loud and ask who else needs to see it.
closeSteps: Recap the pain and the specific value shown; send a tailored one-pager + recap email same day.
Deliver the quote/proposal with a clear implementation picture and start options (pilot campus, grade band).
Identify the procurement path (PO, board approval, purchasing co-op/ESC) and remove friction.
Set the next step with a date every time — never leave a call without one.
On win: warm handoff to onboarding; on stall: return to nurture timed to the budget cycle.

sharedObjections.objection: We already have a platform for that.
sharedObjections.response: Great — we work alongside what you own; we're not asking you to replace it. We make that investment work harder by closing the gap between data and action. Worth 20 minutes to see where we fit?

sharedObjections.objection: Teachers are already overwhelmed with tools.
sharedObjections.response: That's exactly why we built this to remove work, not add it — less grading, less prep, faster next steps. Can I show you the teacher workflow in a short demo?
Understood. Many districts fund this through Title/curriculum/PD/local budgets and start at one campus or grade band. What's your planning window for next year?
Happy to — I'll send a one-pager. It'll land better if I tailor it to your priority; is it more instructional response or STAAR writing? A 15-minute call beats a PDF every time.

products.name: Crystal Instruction
products.oneLiner: The instructional response partner for Texas classrooms.
products.cardBlurb: Instructional tools that turn insight into action.

overview.repHeadline: Sell the instructional response — not another resource library.
overview.repSub: Crystal Instruction helps Texas teachers turn student performance into the next instructional move — without adding prep, grading, or another disconnected system.
overview.badge: Built for Texas classrooms · TEKS aligned
overview.fifteenSecond: Crystal Instruction connects assessment data, standards-aligned instruction, real-time visibility, and built-in next steps so teachers can respond faster — with less work.

buyers.label: The problem
buyers.text: Schools have data. Teachers still have to figure out what to do next.

buyers.label: The difference
buyers.text: Crystal closes the gap between seeing a need and responding instructionally.

buyers.label: The outcome
buyers.text: Teachers get time back. Students receive support before gaps grow.

overview.modelRepNote: Keep this simple. The value is the connected cycle — not any single tool.
overview.isntUseWhen: A buyer asks whether Crystal replaces existing curriculum or systems.

leadWith.heading: The buyer's pain is not a lack of data. It's the work required to turn that data into action.
leadWith.sub: Features prove the story. They are never the opening line.

msgExtras.headline: Find the right message for the person in the room.
msgExtras.sub: Choose an audience to see the lead message, buyer priorities, and language to use.

repAngles.teacher: Focus on time back, clarity in the moment, and support that keeps the teacher in control.
repAngles.campus: Focus on consistency across classrooms and visibility that doesn't add walkthrough burden.
repAngles.district: Focus on scale inside their existing ecosystem — MTSS alignment and measurable growth outcomes.

pbExtras.headline: Know what to say — and what to ask next.
pbExtras.sub: Choose a sales moment to get the script, talking points, objections, and discovery guidance you need.

products.mission: Helping teachers turn student performance into instructional action — in real time and without extra work.
products.positioning: Crystal Instruction is the instructional response teachers use to support student growth based on assessment and classroom data — without extra work. It works alongside a school's existing curriculum, assessments, and MTSS systems to help teachers respond faster to student needs across Math, ELAR, and Science.
products.voice: Bold, energetic, and confident — the magenta personality. Crystal talks in verbs and speed: identify, respond, reteach, act. It validates the district's existing investments, then raises the bar on what teachers can do faster because Crystal exists.

valuePillars.title: Faster instructional response
valuePillars.body: Close the gap between data and action.

valuePillars.title: Real-time visibility
valuePillars.body: Live checkpoints and Crystal Analysis, no grading required.

valuePillars.title: Teacher-led instruction
valuePillars.body: Supports the teacher; it is not more screen time.

valuePillars.title: Targeted support
valuePillars.body: Reteach pathways, SNAP Lessons, and ClearSheets for differentiation.

valuePillars.title: Reduced teacher workload
valuePillars.body: Less analyzing, regrouping, and reteach planning.

products.problem: Schools already have curriculum, assessments, intervention systems, and dashboards. The challenge is no longer collecting data or accessing resources — it's instructional response. Teachers still struggle to identify gaps quickly, know what to reteach, respond immediately, and connect data to immediate action.
products.problemTagline: The challenge isn't data. It's being able to implement next steps with ease.

steps.name: Identify the gap
steps.body: Assessment data (Benchmark, Interim, MAP, STAAR, curriculum) reveals where students need support.

steps.name: Teach
steps.body: Teachers launch targeted, TEKS-aligned support based on that data — without the prep.

steps.name: See
steps.body: Teachers monitor understanding in real time through live checkpoints. No grading required.

steps.name: Respond
steps.body: Teachers know the next instructional move immediately and act on it during instruction.

steps.name: Better outcomes
steps.body: Gaps close before they grow; teachers move on confident students are ready.

isIsnt.is: Instructional response infrastructure that connects assessment data, standard-aligned instruction, real-time insight, and targeted next steps into one streamlined workflow — supporting Tier 1, MTSS, small groups, and intervention.
isIsnt.isnt: A curriculum replacement, a supplemental-content library, a practice/worksheet platform, or an MTSS platform. We support MTSS — we don't become it.

logoRules: The gem mark may appear on white or on black; keep magenta vivid — never muddy or pastel it.
“Crystal” is magenta; “Instruction” is gray. Don't recolor the wordmark.
Endorse with ClearK12: “Crystal Instruction, a ClearK12 product.”
tools: Pathways, SNAP Lessons, ClearSheets (targeted reteach & differentiation)

audienceMessaging.leadMessage: Know what to do next — in real time, without extra work.
Consistent, visible instructional response in every classroom.
Turn student performance into instructional action — at scale, inside your current ecosystem.

sayThis: We work alongside your existing curriculum…
Helps teachers respond before gaps become bigger problems.
Instructional response — not more content.
Supports MTSS and Tier 1.
No grading required to see understanding.
notThis: One connected system / complete workflow platform
bestFitSignals: Active MTSS/RTI focus, Tier 1 or reteach initiatives, or a data-to-action gap.
Already invested in curriculum, benchmarks (MAP, Interim), and dashboards — but next steps are cumbersome, inconsistent, and often independent.
Growth-goal pressure: T-TESS, SLOs, TIA, or accountability improvement.
discoveryQuestions: After a benchmark or unit assessment, what happens next in a typical classroom? How long until students get targeted support?
How consistent is instructional response and reteach from classroom to classroom?
How is MTSS/Tier 1 going — where does execution slip?
products.hook: Crystal Instruction helps teachers respond before learning gaps become bigger problems.

scripts.coldCall: Hi, this is [Your name] with Crystal Instruction. I'll be quick — most Texas schools already have the data and the curriculum; the hard part is what happens right after a benchmark, when teachers have to figure out exactly what students missed and respond before the gap grows. Crystal Instruction works alongside what you already use to help teachers pinpoint the gap, check understanding live, and reteach on the spot. Would a 20-minute demo be worth it to see how it fits your campus?

coldEmail.subject: Responding faster to learning gaps at [District]
coldEmail.body:
Hi [Name] — schools already have curriculum, benchmarks, and dashboards. The hard part is what happens next: identifying exactly what students missed and responding before the gap grows.

Crystal Instruction is the instructional response partner for Texas classrooms. It works alongside what you already use to help teachers reteach the right TEKS, check understanding live (no grading), and act immediately — supporting Tier 1 and MTSS while saving teacher time.

Open to a 20-minute look at how this fits [District]? — [Rep]

pitchOutline: Problem (45s): schools have data and resources; the gap is instructional response — turning performance into immediate action.
Who we are (45s): the instructional response partner; we work alongside existing curriculum — additive, not a replacement.
Teach. See. Respond. (1 min): launch targeted support, see understanding live, respond with the next move.
Scenario (75s): 3rd-grade multiplication — 12 students struggle; Crystal identifies TEKS gaps instantly and launches targeted support without building from scratch.
District & campus impact (1 min): faster response, consistency across classrooms, MTSS support, T-TESS/TIA growth goals.
Close (15s): most programs stop at content, practice, or assessment; Crystal helps teachers know what to do next — in real time.

objections.response: No — Crystal works alongside your curriculum and assessments to help teachers respond faster. We support MTSS; we don't replace it.

objections.objection: How is this different from our data dashboard?
objections.response: Dashboards show what happened. Crystal helps teachers act on it immediately — launch targeted reteach, check understanding live, and move on with confidence.

outreachCadence.leadMessage: Consistent, visible instructional response in every classroom.
outreachCadence.tonePrinciple: Confident, collaborative partner — never salesy or pushy. We respect that principals know their campus better than we do. We offer, we don't corner: every touch has a low-friction ask and a graceful out. No assumptive closes, no pressure, no hype.
outreachCadence.summary: 7-touch, 22-day sequence tuned for principals. Every touch leads with a challenge principals recognize — inconsistent reteach and limited visibility after benchmarks — framed as a partnership, not a pitch. Placeholders: [Name], [Campus], [District], [Rep], [number], [link].
coachingNotes: Tone is partner, not vendor. We're confident collaborators who assume the principal is the expert on their campus. Invite their perspective before offering ours.
Lead with consistency and visibility, not features. Principals care about the outcome of every classroom responding the same way.
Always reinforce 'works alongside your curriculum' and 'in partnership with your teachers' — never another disruptive rollout, never around the teacher.
Give a graceful out in every ask ('if it's useful', 'you're the best judge', 'no pressure'). Offering beats cornering.
Most calls hit voicemail; the voicemail + email pairing is the real play. Try early mornings and right after dismissal.
Keep the guardrails: never say all-in-one, never say replaces your curriculum, never say we are your MTSS platform.

steps.purpose: First touch. Attempt a live call; leave a voicemail; send the email same day so they can consider it on their own time.

components.label: Cold call script
components.body: Hi [Name], this is [Rep] with Crystal Instruction — I'll keep this short out of respect for your time. I partner with Texas principals on something you know better than anyone: after a benchmark, how consistently each classroom responds to what the data shows. Some teachers reteach the right TEKS right away, others take longer. Crystal Instruction helps every teacher respond faster — targeted, TEKS-aligned reteach and live checks for understanding, no grading — and it works alongside the curriculum you already use, so it's not another thing to roll out. I'd genuinely like to hear how your campus handles that today, and show you what we do if it's useful. Would a 20-minute conversation be worthwhile?

components.label: Voicemail
components.body: Hi [Name], this is [Rep] with Crystal Instruction. We partner with Texas campuses to make instructional response more consistent across classrooms — faster reteach after assessments, without more work for teachers. I'll follow up by email so you can look on your own time; if it's helpful to talk, I'm at [number]. Thanks, [Name].

components.label: Gatekeeper / front office
components.body: Hi, I'm hoping you can help me — I'm trying to reach [Name] about supporting your teachers' reteach and intervention after benchmarks. Is there a good way to get 20 minutes on their calendar, or is there someone on the instructional side you'd suggest I start with?

components.label: Fallback email
subjectVariants: Consistent reteach in every classroom at [Campus]
After the benchmark — how consistent is the response?
A partner for your teachers' reteach (not another platform)
components.body:
Hi [Name] — you know your campus better than anyone, so you've likely already felt this: after a benchmark, the gap usually isn't the data, it's how consistently each classroom responds to it. Some teachers reteach the right TEKS right away, others take a week.

Crystal Instruction helps every teacher respond faster — targeted, TEKS-aligned reteach, live checks for understanding (no grading), and a clear next step — while working alongside the curriculum you already use. We see ourselves as a partner to your instructional team, not another platform to manage.

If it's useful, I'd like to learn how your campus approaches this and share what we've seen work elsewhere. Open to a 20-minute conversation?
— [Rep]

variant.label: A/B — short version
variant.subject: A quick question about reteach at [Campus]
variant.body:
Hi [Name] — quick one, no pitch. When a benchmark comes back, how consistent is the reteach from classroom to classroom? That's the challenge we help campuses work through — alongside your current curriculum and in partnership with your teachers, without adding to their plates. If it's worth a short conversation, I'd welcome it.
— [Rep]

steps.purpose: Value follow-up + the self-guided demo. Give one concrete scenario, hand over the async demo link, then leave the decision with them.

components.label: Value email
subjectVariants: One less thing on your teachers' plates
From data to reteach in the same day
components.body:
Hi [Name] — a concrete example of what I meant, and then I'll leave it with you. A 3rd-grade teacher finishes a unit and 12 students miss key TEKS. Normally that's hours of analyzing, regrouping, and hunting for reteach. With Crystal, those gaps surface right away and the teacher can respond the same day — without building anything from scratch.

Across a campus, that's more consistent response in every room and teachers who feel supported rather than buried. If that fits where you're headed this year, I'd enjoy walking your team through it — 20 minutes, whenever suits you. If you'd rather explore on your own first, here's a self-guided demo — no scheduling required: crystalinstruction.com/self-guided-demo
— [Rep]

variant.label: A/B — outcome-led
variant.subject: What consistent reteach could look like at [Campus]
variant.body:
Hi [Name] — picture walking any classroom the week after a benchmark and seeing the same thing everywhere: teachers already reteaching the exact TEKS students missed and checking understanding live. That consistency is what we help campuses build — alongside your current curriculum, and in partnership with your teachers, not around them. Happy to show you if it's useful; you're the best judge of the fit.
— [Rep]

Want to look on your own first? Self-guided demo: crystalinstruction.com/self-guided-demo

steps.purpose: Second call attempt. Reference the emails, invite their perspective, and offer to work around their schedule — no assumptive close.

components.label: Call talk-track
components.body: Hi [Name], [Rep] with Crystal Instruction — I sent a couple of notes about consistent instructional response after benchmarks. I'd genuinely value your perspective: is that something your campus is working on this year? [pause and listen] If it's useful, I'd like to show your team how it works and hear how you're approaching it now. I can fit a 20-minute conversation whenever works best for you — what does your calendar look like over the next couple of weeks?

components.label: Voicemail
components.body: Hi [Name], [Rep] with Crystal Instruction following up. No pressure at all — if it's useful, I'd like to show you how campuses build more consistent reteach across classrooms, in about 20 minutes. I'll resend my email, or I'm at [number]. Thanks, [Name].

steps.purpose: Lower the effort. Share a short overview so they can self-qualify on their own time; offer a walkthrough only if worthwhile.

components.label: Show-don't-tell email
subjectVariants: Something to look at on your own time
How other Texas campuses handle reteach
components.body:
Hi [Name] — figured it's easier to show than tell, and this way you can look whenever suits you. Here's a 2-minute overview of how Crystal helps teachers turn assessment results into targeted reteach: [link].

Campuses in [similar Texas district/ESC] use it to keep instructional response consistent from classroom to classroom. If it looks relevant, I'd be glad to walk through it with your team, tailored to your grade bands — only if it's worth your time.
— [Rep]

steps.purpose: Third call. Try early morning or right after dismissal; tie to an upcoming window and make it easy to say 'not now.'

components.label: Call talk-track
components.body: Hi [Name], [Rep] with Crystal Instruction. I know your mornings are about the only quiet moment, so I'll be brief. I think there could be a real fit here for supporting your teachers' response after assessments — but you're the best judge of that. If you're open to it, I'd welcome a 20-minute conversation before your next benchmark window, on your schedule. And if the timing isn't right, just say so and I'll step back.

components.label: Voicemail
components.body: Hi [Name], [Rep] again with Crystal Instruction — trying you early since afternoons are full. Same idea: more consistent instructional response across classrooms, in partnership with your teachers and without more work for them. I'm at [number] if it's useful — and no worries at all if now isn't the time. Thanks, [Name].

steps.purpose: Referral pivot. Offer an easy out and ask to be pointed to the right owner — follow their lead on timing.

components.label: Referral email
subjectVariants: Am I aimed at the right person?
Happy to follow your lead here
components.body:
Hi [Name] — I don't want to keep filling your inbox, and I may simply have this pointed at the wrong person. If instructional response and reteach across classrooms sits more with your C&I or assessment lead, I'd be grateful for a pointer their way.

And if it is you but the timing's just off, tell me when to circle back — I'm happy to follow your lead. Either way, thank you for reading.
— [Rep]

steps.purpose: Step back gracefully. Close the loop with warmth, leave the door open, and seed the spring planning window.

components.label: Step-back email
subjectVariants: Stepping back for now — door's open
components.body:
Hi [Name] — I'll pause here so I'm not a distraction during a busy stretch. If it's ever useful: Crystal Instruction helps campuses make reteach faster and more consistent across every classroom, in partnership with your teachers and alongside what you already use.

Spring is when a lot of campuses plan for next year, so I'll plan to check back around then unless you'd like to talk sooner. Whatever you decide, I'm rooting for you and your teachers this year.
— [Rep]

products.name: LoneStar CR
products.oneLiner: The constructed-response writing partner for Texas classrooms.
products.cardBlurb: Writing practice and instant scoring built for STAAR growth.
products.marketTagline: A system for developing strong writers.
products.mission: Helping teachers grow confident writers — by making writing practice frequent, scaffolded, and instantly scored, without the grading burden.
products.positioning: LoneStar CR is the constructed-response writing partner for Texas classrooms. Using advanced AI, teachers create standards-aligned ECR and SCR tasks in seconds, students write with individualized scaffolding, and every response is scored instantly with personalized feedback — built exclusively for TEKS and Texas writing.
products.voice: Confident, credible, and Texas-proud — the same energy as Crystal, in teal. LoneStar CR leans on evidence: it pairs bold, benefit-led claims ('Write smarter, learn faster') with a strong research spine. It speaks plainly to the grading pain teachers feel every week and to the STAAR outcomes leaders own.

valuePillars.title: More writing practice
valuePillars.body: Students write far more when every response no longer waits on hand-grading.

valuePillars.title: Instant scoring & feedback
valuePillars.body: Students get a grade and personalized feedback immediately; teachers stop hand-grading.

valuePillars.title: Built for standards
valuePillars.body: ECR/SCR aligned to what the state actually requires.

valuePillars.title: Automated, multi-tiered scaffolding
valuePillars.body: Support auto-adjusts to each student's level.

valuePillars.title: Time back for teachers
valuePillars.body: Teachers stop hand-grading every response.

products.problem: It is unsustainable for teachers to grade writing and provide feedback in a way that is timely and meaningful for students. Students need far more writing reps than they get, because every response a teacher assigns is another stack to grade by hand. So practice stays low, feedback comes days late, and writing scores lag. The bottleneck isn't willingness — it's grading time.
products.problemTagline: The bottleneck to better writing isn't effort — it's grading time.

steps.name: Assign
steps.body: Teachers access unlimited standards-aligned ECR/SCR tasks in seconds, with strategy anchors like RACE built in.

steps.name: Write
steps.body: Students write with automated scaffolding matched to their level, plus self-paced lessons on writing fundamentals.

steps.name: Score
steps.body: AI scores every response instantly and returns personalized, actionable feedback — no manual grading.

steps.name: Revise & Grow
steps.body: Students revise across drafts (where the research shows the biggest gains) while teachers watch growth in Crystal-Clear reports.

isIsnt.is: A specialist writing-practice and instant-scoring tool that dramatically increases practice volume and returns teacher time.
isIsnt.isnt: A full ELAR curriculum, a general essay checker, or a replacement for the teacher's judgment. The teacher stays in control of instruction; LoneStar CR removes the grading bottleneck.

logoRules: Lead with the Texas-outline mark; keep the star/state silhouette recognizable — don't crop it.
Use the white/blue reversed logo on deep-teal backgrounds; full color on white.
Endorse with ClearK12: “LoneStar CR, a ClearK12 product.”

audienceMessaging.leadMessage: Assign more writing, grade none of it — and give every student instant feedback.
More writing practice in every classroom — with visible, tracked growth.
A Texas-built, research-backed way to raise constructed-response scores at scale.

sayThis: Built exclusively for TEKS and Texas writing.
Assign more writing, grade none of it.
More writing practice, without more grading.
Instant, personalized feedback on every response.
Research-backed writing growth in daily routines.
notThis: Works for any state / any test
Grades like a human, so no need to review
bestFitSignals: STAAR writing/constructed-response performance is a named priority (ECR/SCR).
ELAR teachers overloaded with writing grading; low writing-practice volume.
Grades 3-8 and EOC English focus; interest in AI that's TEKS-specific and Texas-built.
discoveryQuestions: How is constructed response (ECR/SCR) going on STAAR — and in daily practice?
Realistically, how often do students write, and how long until they get feedback?
How much time are ELAR teachers spending grading writing each week?
products.proofIntro: Frequent writing, explicit strategies, and instant feedback across drafts map directly to the research base. Use these to substantiate claims; cite responsibly.
proofPoints: Strategy-rich writing instruction improved achievement by up to +1.14 SD (Graham & Perin, Writing Next, 2007).
Automated writing evaluation produced a +0.86 SD effect, strongest when students revised across drafts (Zhai & Ma, 2023).
Immediate automated feedback improved writing performance by +0.55 SD across grade levels (Fleckenstein et al., 2023).
Daily argument writing raised scores +0.21 on a 1-6 rubric across 47 districts (SRI, C3WP, 2017).
products.productClaims: Instant scoring and personalized feedback on every response, built exclusively for TEKS and Texas writing, grounded in the peer-reviewed research above.
products.hook: Assign more writing, grade none of it — and give every student instant feedback.

scripts.coldCall: Hi, this is ___ with LoneStar CR. The quick reason I'm calling is that the redesigned STAAR made constructed-response writing central — and it's the hardest thing to practice at volume, because every response a teacher assigns is another stack to grade. LoneStar CR is built exclusively for TEKS and Texas writing. Teachers create ECR and SCR tasks in seconds, students write with scaffolding matched to their level, and every response is scored instantly with personalized feedback — so students write far more, and teachers get their grading time back. Would it be worth a quick 20-minute demo to see how this could work for your ELAR teachers?

coldEmail.subject: More writing practice, zero grading, at [District]
coldEmail.body:
Hi [Name] — STAAR now leans hard on constructed response (ECR/SCR), but students can only write as much as teachers can grade. That's the bottleneck.

LoneStar CR is the constructed-response writing partner for Texas classrooms. Built for TEKS, it lets teachers generate unlimited writing tasks, gives students instant AI scoring and personalized feedback (with RACE/TAG strategy anchors and auto-scaffolding), and takes the grading stack off teachers' desks — so students can write far more, far more often.

Worth a 20-minute look for [District]? — [Rep]

pitchOutline: Problem (45s): STAAR made ECR/SCR central; practice is capped by grading time; feedback comes days late.
Who we are (45s): the constructed-response writing partner, built exclusively for TEKS and Texas writing — a specialist, not a curriculum.
Write. Score. Grow. (1 min): assign in seconds, students write with scaffolding, instant scoring + feedback, revise across drafts.
Proof (1 min): frequent writing, explicit strategies, and instant feedback are the research-backed levers (Writing Next; AWE meta-analyses).
Impact (1 min): dramatically more practice, grading time returned to teachers, tracked growth in Crystal-Clear reports.
Close (15s): more writing, none of the grading — the fastest path to better Texas writers.

objections.objection: Can AI really grade writing accurately?
objections.response: It scores instantly and consistently against the rubric, and teachers stay in control — you can review and adjust. The win is volume and speed of feedback, where the research shows the biggest gains.

objections.objection: Is this just an essay checker like the free tools?
objections.response: No — it's built exclusively for TEKS and Texas ECR/SCR, with auto-scaffolding, strategy anchors like RACE/TAG, and district analytics. Generic tools aren't aligned to what STAAR requires.

crossSell.leadWithCrystal: Overall instructional response, MTSS/Tier 1, reteach, and multi-subject data-to-action (Math, ELAR, Science).
crossSell.leadWithLonestar: STAAR writing performance, constructed response (ECR/SCR), the ELAR grading burden, or writing practice volume.
crossSell.combinedStory: ClearK12 helps your teachers respond faster to what students need — across every subject with Crystal Instruction, and specifically on the writing that STAAR now demands with LoneStar CR. Both are built for Texas, both save teachers time, and both work alongside what you already own.
